institutionName: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
collegeName: College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)
programName: Bachelor of Science in Biological Engineering — Natural Resources
tagline: Engineer Solutions for Water, Soil & Ecosystems
programDescription: The Natural Resources concentration in Biological Engineering focuses on engineering solutions for water quality, soil conservation, watershed management, and ecological restoration. Students apply engineering design to environmental challenges including stormwater management, erosion control, and water treatment. The ABET-accredited program produces engineers ready to address the most pressing environmental infrastructure needs.
accreditationBody: ABET (Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology)

certifications: Certified Erosion, Sediment & Storm Water Inspector (CESSWI)
careerOutlook.outlook: Environmental engineers are increasingly critical as communities face climate adaptation, aging water infrastructure, and stricter environmental regulations. The $96K median salary and 6% growth make this one of the highest-paying and most stable engineering fields.
